contexts.context: ...a love poem, entitled, "The Beloved of <mark>Dannisich</mark>." To render it intelligible, we are informed...
...adorned with coins; but I love thee, <mark>Dannisich</mark>, better than both.
...of a young woman from the mountains; but <mark>Dannisich</mark>, thou art to me more beautiful than either...
...his breath was sweet as violets; but when <mark>Dannisich</mark> kissed me, my heart thrilled with pleasure.
...but they must be the songs and guzla of <mark>Dannisich</mark>.
...father; but come thou under my window, <mark>Dannisich</mark>, and I will fly with thee.'
